items.title: Organisations lose sight of employee AI use, report finds
items.summary: More firms are using AI daily, but AvePoint found unauthorised access incidents remain widespread as governance trails behind adoption.

items.title: Risky Business #844 -- China closes AI vulndev gap as USA lifts Fable ban
items.summary: On this week’s show Patrick Gray, Adam Boileau and James Wilson discuss the week’s cybersecurity news. They cover: Anthropic’s Fable 5 returning while OpenAI’s GPT-5.6 gets thrown in model jail Distillation, cheap tokens, and AI chat harvesting is an industry in China Edge becomes a lolbin via a new

items.title: AI does not invent cyber risk, it accelerates it
items.summary: Organisations have only days to patch gaps as AI-driven attackers automate the same old weaknesses, Five Eyes warned.

items.title: AI is already running inside the enterprise. Is Australia ready?
items.summary: Australian firms are using AI at scale, but many lack the visibility to stop shadow tools, agentic access and rising incidents.

items.title: Australian banks lag on strict DMARC email protection
items.summary: Most customers still face avoidable phishing risk, as 59% of Australian banks lack the strict DMARC setting that blocks spoofed emails.

items.title: Google Cloud adds long-lookback PromQL alert policies
items.summary: History-based alerts in Cloud Monitoring can now compare workloads with patterns stretching back two years, reducing false alarms from fixed thresholds.

items.title: Asia firms adopt agentic AI faster than resilience
items.summary: Recovery plans are lagging as Asian companies rush into agentic AI, with average incident downtime stretching to 28 days, a survey found.

items.title: China-Linked Group Targets Southeast Asia Critical Systems
items.summary: The group compromised at least 10 regional organizations, including two state-owned entities, and deployed a new backdoor.

items.title: SMS Sender ID Register goes live to help protect Australians from scams
items.summary: The Australian Communications and Media Authority (ACMA) says its new SMS Sender ID Register has commenced, in a move aimed at reducing text message impersonation scams by making it clearer [...]

items.title: Fake Bug Report Hijacks AI Coding Agents at Scale
items.summary: "Agentjacking" is the latest demonstration of how easily attackers can exploit an AI agent's inability to differentiate between content and instructions.

items.title: Phishers Gain Persistence at EU, Asia Hospitality Orgs
items.summary: Separate but similar campaigns described by Microsoft and Trend Micro use malicious zip files to spread malware via social engineering and obsfucation, including blockchain abuse.

items.title: County With 37 Data Centers Asks Schools to ‘Conserve Electricity’
items.summary: Henrico County is a major hub for data centers in Virginia. Its officials said it expects a 25% rise in electricity costs next year, and advised workers to close the blinds and turn off their computers to make up for it.

items.title: Scammers Sell Seeds for Exotic AI-Generated Flowers That Don’t Exist
items.summary: Ebay, Amazon, and Etsy are unable to stop the flood of AI-generated seed scams.

items.title: Companies Are Making Claude and Codex Talk Like Cavemen to Stop AI’s Soaring Costs
items.summary: A senior OpenAI employee has contributed code to the project, simply called 'caveman.'

items.title: AI-Generated Workflows Are a Silent Security Disaster
items.summary: Teams are dealing with a truly dangerous problem — automation that works, but that no one understands.

items.title: NIST Enrichment Reductions Impact CVE Coverage, Accuracy
items.summary: The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) scaled back the number of CVEs it selects for in-depth analysis, but the move has produced mixed results, according to researchers.

items.title: 'Djinn' Stealer Targets Cloud, AI Credentials
items.summary: The infostealer was delivered via CVE-2026-48558, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in SimpleHelp, targeting credentials linking development and admin environments to wider enterprise systems.

items.title: Vulnerabilities Expose Private Data in Indian Government Systems
items.summary: One critical vulnerability, among many discovered by a researcher, could have allowed anyone to walk in and take over a national government portal.

items.title: Iran, Russia, China Target Water Systems for Sabotage
items.summary: Nation-state attackers breach water systems through weak passwords, exposed PLCs, and poor segmentation — not sophisticated malware.

items.title: Amazon Q VS Extension Flaw Leads to Cloud Credential Theft
items.summary: Adversaries could plant a malicious repository that can execute arbitrary code and steal cloud credentials by exploiting the vulnerability, which showcases growing MCP risk.

items.title: Third-Party Breaches Teach Education Sector a Costly Lesson in Vendor Risk
items.summary: Rising threats from third-party actors are forcing institutions to play defense to protect student data from ransomware and other attacks.

items.title: New Initiative Tackles Security for End-of-Life Open Source Software
items.summary: The Open Source Sustainability Initiative's goal is to help enterprises manage and secure aging open source projects while maintaining regulatory compliance.

items.summary: The FSB state-sponsored operation has gotten a lot better at loading its malware and hiding its servers.

items.title: EdTech Attackers Shift From Schools to Their Software Suppliers
items.summary: Educational institutions, the edtech companies they rely on, and, more concerningly, the challenges they pose for schools are the focus of the latest Reporters' Notebook video series.

items.title: Local Police Collusion Hampers Crackdown on Asian Scam Centers
items.summary: With tens of billions of dollars flowing into regional economies from cybercrime, scam centers continue to flourish, despite international and law-enforcement efforts.

items.title: Bodycam Shows Moment Cops Arrested a Man for Speaking Too Long at Data Center Meeting
items.summary: We spoke to Darren Blanchard, the man arrested while speaking out against data centers at a community meeting. He's sharing the bodycam footage of his arrest for the first time with 404 Media.

items.title: Europe Evolves Into Ransomware's Favorite Region
items.summary: After a global lull, ransomware gangs are setting sights on a rich new arena: attacking EU organizations and their suppliers.

items.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Faces Surge in Cyber Threats
items.summary: Persistent cybercrime, social engineering, and infrastructure threats continue to plague the FIFA 2026 World Cup across the US, Canada, and Mexico.

items.title: Podcast: If AI Is Sentient Then So Is 'Age of Empires II'
items.summary: A surreal but compelling LLM experiment with Age of Empires II; how a Texas city sold land meant for a park to a data center company; and the Madison Square Garden hack.

items.title: Apple's MacOS Gap Lets Users Disable Security Tools
items.summary: Attackers can exploit the issue to disable security and integrated browser tools without needing administrator privileges or kernel exploits.

items.title: ASD to retire Essential Eight within two years, consults on replacement
items.summary: The Australian Signals Directorate and the Australian Cyber Security Centre say the Essential Eight cyber security framework will be retired within the next two years and replaced by a new [...]

items.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
items.summary: Two men pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom this week to criminal charges stemming from an August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London, the entity responsible for the public transport network in the Greater London area. The duo were key members of a prolific cybercrime group known as

items.title: SocGholish Takedown Highlights Malicious TDS Threats
items.summary: SocGholish uses traffic distribution systems (TDSs) to provide initial access into victims' networks for cybercrime groups such as the notorious Evil Corp.

items.title: FortiBleed Attackers Turn Firewalls Into Credential Stealers as Heists Persist
items.summary: The threat actors engineered a Golang-based sniffer to target 430,000 FortiGate firewalls and identify 110 million credentials in the ongoing global campaign.

items.summary: The Australian Computer Society (ACS) has appointed Dr Prins Ralston as chief executive officer, effective immediately. ACS said Ralston has been a member for 35 years and previously served as [...]

items.summary: Leaders of the Five Eyes cybersecurity agencies have issued a joint call for organisations to treat artificial intelligence as an immediate driver of cyber risk, warning that AI is increasing [...]

items.title: DifyTap Bugs Let Attackers 'Wiretap' AI Chat Histories
items.summary: Four vulnerabilities allow attackers to exploit Dify, a platform for AI application building and management, to silently access and exfiltrate sensitive data.

items.title: He Thought He Was Secure; His Phone Number Was Stolen Anyway
items.summary: Threat actors can easily steal one-time passwords sent by text when they conduct a SIM swap attack. This can lead to account takeovers, so users must layer up their security measures.

items.summary: Cyberattacks increasingly aimed at civil society organisations are expanding beyond the types of threats traditionally associated with governments and large enterprises, according to new data published by Cloudflare. The company [...]

items.title: ‘Popa’ Botnet Linked to Publicly-Traded Israeli Firm
items.summary: For the past four years, a sprawling Android-based botnet called Popa has forced millions of consumer TV boxes to relay Internet traffic linked to advertising fraud, account takeovers, and mass data-scraping efforts. items.title: Salesforce Data Thefts Continue via Klue App Compromise
items.summary: Klue's Battlecards is now the third integrated application that has been compromised to steal customers' Salesforce data, and victims include Huntress, the cybersecurity vendor.

items.title: Aon study finds Australia facing AI skills and benefits gaps as adoption rises
items.summary: A new study by Aon has found Australian organisations are adopting artificial intelligence at pace but lagging in workforce readiness, with gaps in skills availability, employee benefits and pay transparency. [...]

items.title: Healthcare cyber investment rises, but confidence in recovery lags: Wasabi survey
items.summary: Australian healthcare organisations are increasing spending on cyber resilience and data protection, but many IT professionals remain unsure they could keep critical data operational after a cyberattack, according to the [...]

items.title: ‘Digital duty of care’ push puts AI accountability in focus
items.summary: Following the release of a new Federal Government discussion paper, calls are growing for stronger safeguards to ensure artificial intelligence is used responsibly across workplaces and society. The paper proposes [...]

items.title: INC Ransomware Thrives by Mastering the Basics
items.summary: And one of those basics is focusing on sectors where a ransomware disruption creates immediate pressure to pay up, like with healthcare.

items.title: Sweeping Credential-Harvesting Heist Compromises 30K+ Fortinet Devices
items.summary: Attackers are actively targeting various sectors across nearly 200 countries and already have compiled a list of working credentials for tens of thousands of compromised devices.
